item.title: Smoke test + backing services
item.intent: Post-deploy smoke test must send a real message and get a response — 'container started' is not 'agent works'. Users must not discover a dead agent by noticing silence.
item.description: MUST: The first-deploy user experience is broken without this. 'clawhq up succeeded' when the agent doesn't actually respond is worse than a clear failure — the user wastes time debugging a silently dead agent. Upgrade to must: smoke test (real message + response) is non-negotiable for deploy confidence. Scope split: smoke test (real message exchange) is must; backing services (postgres/redis/qdrant) can be a separate should item if needed. Acceptance: 'clawhq up' ends with a real message sent to agent and response received; failure is loud with actionable fix; 'clawhq service add postgres' configures backing service.
steps: Implement post-deploy smoke test: send real message, verify response
Implement 'clawhq service add' for postgres, redis, qdrant backing services
Make deploy fail loudly if smoke test fails
acceptanceCriteria: 'clawhq up' ends with real message sent to agent and response received
Smoke test failure is loud with actionable fix guidance
'clawhq service add postgres' configures backing service correctly
